programmes.show_description: Eye Witness: Patricia 'Hyacinth Bucket' Routledge stars in the peculiar mystery series. Wainthropp Detective Agency is up and running, and Hetty must find a missing bird-watcher.
Civil War: When a retired policeman is murdered during a Civil War re-enactment, the complicated investigation into his death uncovers three mistresses and a 35-year-old feud.
Lights, Camera, Murder!: A TV crew arrives to use St Vincent's Convent as a location for a spy thriller; the filming becomes all too real when a live gun is fired instead of a replica.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Time and Tides: Comedy from the east - Essex that is. Dorien is in need of all the loving support she can get from Sharon and Tracey; which doesn't sound very likely!
Zero Tolerance: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness.
New. A Step Too Far: Nick and Jimmi come into conflict over the treatment of an agoraphobic mother and Melody discovers JJ is taking illegal drugs.
New. Endless Love: An old school friend of Ruth's tries to track down her father when her mother dies, while Archie struggles with his guilty conscience after discussing JJ with Eva.
Thicker than Water: Ric celebrates his 50th birthday, but tensions are running high at the party, as Jess tries to make Zubin jealous and Connie plays mind games with Will.
Place of Safety: Ruth is sectioned and held in a specialist unit, where consultant psychiatrist Andrew Brookfield tries to pinpoint the cause of her distress.
Zero Tolerance: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness.
New. Zainab's deceit is finally revealed to a devastated Masood. Pat and Peggy each show off their new gentleman friend to a bewildered Dot.
New. Zainab fights to save her marriage as Tamwar makes a confession. Billy resorts to desperate measures to make ends meet and the identity of the Blue Masquerader is revealed.
Where Sheep May Safely Graze: Yorkshire countryside and intriguing veterinary dilemmas are on the cards. Alfred, the beloved cat of local confectioner Geoff Hatfield, falls ill.
Avril confronts Ken and Lynne returns home. Meanwhile, Sir Edward reveals shocking news about his health.
Fingers: Patricia Routledge leaves Hyacinth Bucket behind in the compelling mystery series. Hetty takes on a new case while on holiday with Robert. But who is she working for?
Baby Come Back: More jokes about Essex girls in the popular sitcom. When Sharon meets Dave, the telephone engineer and old flame, her thoughts turn to marriage and motherhood.
Jubilee: Vintage laughs from the three old fellas in the show that seems to be immortal. Foggy wants to do something for the Royal Jubilee, but things, as ever, don't go to plan.
Flower Power Cut: The loveable elderly trio of Yorkshiremen get into more trouble as Clegg is caught behaving oddly in the cafe.
Who Made a Bit of a Splash in Wales, Then?: Much-cherished sitcom. A trip to the valleys for the youthful codgers, as Foggy pays a visit to his lady friend.
New. Sparring with Death: Final Chapter: A second death at the Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat leads Mattie to uncover a heart-wrenching tragedy, waiting to be avenged.
The Devil You Know: Mallory is forced to turn to the sleuthing priest for help when a senior member of the police force is murdered at a bowls match.
Couldn't Organise One: Dennis 'Minder' Waterman stars in the crime drama with a splash of comedy. The team probes a death at a brewery, which proves an intoxicating experience.
Know Thine Enemy: Mystery with the aristocratic detective. Lynley and Havers's friendship is pushed to its limits in a case probing the disappearance of two teen girls.
Fingers: Patricia Routledge leaves Hyacinth Bucket behind in the compelling mystery series. Hetty takes on a new case while on holiday with Robert. But who is she working for?
Three Coins and a Fountain: The Brokenwood fountain is spraying reddish-brown water due to a man impaled upon it; the victim is an American tourist who has three gold coins.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Sisters Are Doing It: Comedy Essex-style and Tracey's lecherous new boss is causing problems. Luckily, superwomen Sharon and Dorien are at hand to save the day!
Scoop: Conway becomes involved with a call girl, thanks to Boulton. But is the charming Linda all that she seems?
New. Inside Out: Nick advises Melody she is to undergo more training when she turns up late at work after collecting JJ from the police station.
New. Geraldine Mooney's Departed: Jimmi attempts to help a woman whose dead husband is blocking a hallway, and Vivien prepares for a driving theory test.
Time Waits for No Man: Zubin's jealousy overcomes him, and Diane regrets her decision to move in with Owen. Ric and Will grow closer and Tricia goes too far with Carlos.
Duty of Care: Jordan runs into problems while carrying out a surgical procedure on a Middle Eastern princess, much to the disapproval of Henrik Hanssen.
Scoop: Conway becomes involved with a call girl, thanks to Boulton. But is the charming Linda all that she seems?
New. Ronnie thinks she has found the answer to Jack's prayers. Meanwhile, under pressure, Billy crosses a line.
New. Billy struggles to cope as his life is turned upside down, and Ronnie finds that she is running out of options. Meanwhile, Masood is determined to heal his family.
The New World: Laughs and adventures with the 1930s Yorkshire vets in the nostalgic series. Lady Hutton's sick cat prompts Siegfried into updating the practice's image.
A mysterious stranger arrives in Tarrant as the Howards throw a party for Lynne, and Sir Edward tries to patch things up with Charles.
Widdershins: Engrossing mystery with Patricia Routledge as the lady sleuth, helped by Lost's Dominic Monaghan. Hetty is drawn to a country village by the death of Robert's uncle.
Just Family: Another episode of the classic Essex comedy. Tracey is hurt when Darryl asks her to give up one of her prison visits so that his mum and dad can go and see him.
Greenfingers: Come and visit the three old men of Yorkshire with more gentle fun involving wifely dragons and hare-brained schemes. The trio get passionate about vegetables.
The Bandit from Stoke-on-Trent: Come for a lengthy walk through some lovely countryside with those youthful codgers. An old friend of the trio is back in town, but why?
Full Steam Behind: The world's longest-running sitcom, following three silver chaps in their wanderings through the countryside. A journey by steam train is on the cards for the trio.
The Dance of Death: The amateur sleuth uncovers a dark secret when a contestant is murdered at a ballroom dancing competition.
The gripping conclusion to the acclaimed drama series about a Yorkshire police sergeant. Catherine's fears grow when she learns that Tommy has been spending time with Ryan.
Magic Majestic: Crime drama with the amiable ageing detectives, including ex-Likely Lad James Bolam. A session with the Magic Circle's Dr Moroni leaves Gerry feeling paranoid.
A Clubbable Woman: The very first episode in the cop drama series starring Warren Clarke, from the novels of Reginald Hill. The wife of a rugby star is found murdered at her home.
